segments.s436-g41: ازو is a fused form of از + او meaning “of him/her” and functions as the genitive/agent marker before یادگاری. یادگاری (memento/inscription) is the thing being made; کنم is the 1st‑person singular present/subjunctive of کردن used here with a future/volitive sense (“I will make” / “I shall set up”). در جهان is an adverbial phrase (“in the world”) placed after the verb phrase to indicate the scope/location of the remembrance.
segments.s436-g42: که introduces the subordinate clause giving the intended result/purpose (“so that” / “that”). تا هست = “as long as there is / while there exist(s)” (تا here = until/so long as, هست = exist). مردم is the subject “people,” and نگردد is the verb ن + گردد (subjunctive/passive sense) meaning “does not become / will not become.” نهان is the predicative adjective “hidden”; the clause means “that, while people exist, he will not become hidden.”
segments.s436-g43: بدین نامه is a prepositional/agentive phrase: بدین (ب + دین fused here to mean “by/with this”) + نامه “letter.” It functions adverbially: “by this letter / with this letter.” شهریاران پیش places شهریاران (“kings/lords”) before the postposition پیش meaning “in the presence of / before,” so together it reads “before the kings.”
segments.s436-g44: This is a parallel phrase: بزرگان (“the great ones / nobles”) followed by و (“and”), then جنگی as an adjectival/epithet meaning “warlike” applied to سواران (“horsemen/riders”), and finally پیش as the postposition “before/in the presence of.” The whole segment lists audiences before whom the letter is presented.
segments.s436-g45: همه is the general subject/particle “all / everything.” رزم و بزمست pairs two nouns, but بزمست is the noun بزم plus the copula -است contracted (بزم + است → بزمست), literally “(it) is a feast,” so the line reads “all is battle and feast.” The following و رای و سخن continues the list: رای “counsel/plan” and سخن “speech/discourse.” The segment is a compact poetic coordination equating the world with conflict, festivity, counsel and speech.
segments.s436-g46: گذشته is the adjective/participial “past / gone” used predicatively. بسی here is an adverbial intensifier (“very / many / much”) qualifying روزگار (“time/ages”), and کهن is an adjective “ancient.” Word order is typical: modifier phrases after the verb/participle, giving “many ancient times have passed” (the verb ‘have passed’ is implicit in the participle گذشته).
segments.s436-g47: همان is a demonstrative/adjective meaning “the same” or “that very.” It modifies a coordinate list: دانش “learning/knowledge,” و دین “and religion/faith,” و پرهیز “and restraint/forbearance,” و رای “and counsel/plan.” The segment lists virtues/qualities, all prefixed by همان to indicate they are the same as previously mentioned.
segments.s436-g48: همان again repeats “the same” and modifies رهنمونی (رهنمونی = guidance/instruction). به is the preposition “to/for,” دیگری (دیگر) means “other/another,” and سرای here denotes a realm/house/abode (often “the other world” in this poetic context). The phrase means “the same guidance to the other realm/afterlife.”
segments.s436-g57: که is the subordinating conjunction 'that' introducing this subordinate clause. از من ('from/of me') marks possession or origin (من takes the oblique sense 'me'). پس از is the temporal phrase 'after' (پس + از) and مرگ is 'death'; ماند is the verb 'remains/is left' (3rd sing.), placed before the noun نشان in poetic word order — literally 'remains a token'.
segments.s436-g58: ز is the poetic variant of از meaning 'from'. گنج is 'treasure' and شهنشاه 'king of kings, emperor' form a genitive chain 'the emperor's treasure'. گردنکشان is a postpositive plural epithet (literally 'neck-raisers') — here 'the proud' or 'rebels' — qualifying the source/owner; the whole phrase reads 'from the emperor's treasure (of the proud-necked).'
segments.s436-g59: کنون 'now' sets the time. بازگردم is 1st-person singular present/optative ('I return' / 'let me return') and here means 'I turn back' or 'I resume'. به + گفتار is the prepositional object 'to the tale/speech' and سرو (literally 'cypress') names the subject of that tale (a poetic epithet for a tall/beautiful person).
segments.s436-g60: فروزنده 'radiant/brightening' and the following words form epithets: سهل ماهان functions here as a compound epithet or proper name (Sahl Mahan) — in classical Persian poetry personal names often appear as adjectival phrases. به مرو is the locative 'to/at Merv' (the city). Grammatically these modifiers attach to 'سرو' in the previous segment, completing 'the tale of the cypress, the radiant Sahl Mahan, at Merv.'
segments.s436-g49: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of'. چیزی is an indefinite 'something'. کزیشان is the fused relative construction کز + ایشان (archaic contraction of که از ایشان) meaning 'which/of them' or 'that is of them'. پسـند here is a noun/predicate meaning 'liking, that which is pleasing' and combines with آمدن (پسند آید) to mean 'to be pleasing'. آیدش is آید + the enclitic ش (to him), so 'it comes to him' = 'it pleases him'. The whole relative clause therefore means 'something of theirs/which is pleasing (to him)'.
segments.s436-g50: همین = 'this very'; روز = 'day'. را is the classical definite/object/topic marker attaching to همین روز. سودمند is the predicate adjective 'beneficial/profitable'. آیدش (as above) = 'comes to him' (i.e. 'is of benefit to him'). Word order is predicate after object marker: 'this very day will come as beneficial to him.'
segments.s436-g51: کزان is the fused prepositional/relative form (کز + آن/از آن) meaning 'from that / of those'. برتران is a plural noun 'the superior ones / nobles' (here the subject). یادگارش = یادگار + possessive ش 'his memorial/remembrance'. بود is the past copula 'was'. The clause literally reads 'From those the nobles were his memorial.'
segments.s436-g52: همان = 'the same' / 'that very (one)'. مونس = 'companion, consoler'. روزگارش = روزگار + ش 'his time / his days'. بود again is the past copula 'was'. The clause says 'that same was the companion of his days' (i.e. it served as his solace/companion through his times).
segments.s436-g53: همی is an emphatic/continuous adverb meaning 'still/always'. چشم دارم is an idiom 'to have an eye' = 'to watch/hope/expect' (دارم is present 'I have'). بدین is the preposition + demonstrative (here split as ب + دین in the word list) meaning 'in/to these'; روزگار = 'time/these times'. Together: 'I continually keep watch/expectation in these times.'
segments.s436-g54: که introduces the purpose/result clause here ('so that / so as to'). دینار = 'dinar, coin (money)'. یابم is the 1st-person present/subjunctive form of یافتن/یابیدن meaning 'may I obtain / I might get' (subjunctive/optative sense). من is emphatic 'I'. از = 'from', شهریار = 'the king'. The clause = 'that I may obtain a dinar from the king.'
segments.s436-g55: دگر here = 'another/else' (or 'again' in some contexts). چشم دارم as before 'I keep my eye / I hope'. به = preposition 'to/toward'. دیگر سرای = 'another/other abode/house' (سرای in classical poetic usage = 'abode, house, (often) the next world' rather than the modern lemma sense provided). The phrase means 'I turn my hope toward another abode.'
segments.s436-g56: که = 'that' introducing a subordinate clause. آمرزش = 'forgiveness/pardon'. آید = present/subjunctive 'may come'. مرا + را here are written separately in the word list (م + را); grammatically this is the object pronoun 'to me' (مرا). از خدای = 'from God'. So the clause = 'that forgiveness may come to me from God.'
segments.s436-g9: بگویم is a 1st-person present/subjunctive form used here as a narrative/future 'I will say' or 'I will tell'. کنون is an adverb 'now' modifying the clause. آنچ ... یافتم is a relative-phrase 'that which I found/learned' where آنچ = 'that which' and ازو = the older fused form for 'از او' ('from him'); یافتم is the past tense 'I found/obtained' and is the object of بگویم.
segments.s436-g10: سخن را marks the direct object (سخن 'speech/words/tale' + را definite object marker). یک اندر دگر is an idiomatic adverbial phrase 'one into another' (اندر = 'in/into', دگر = 'other/another'). بافتم is 1st-person past of بافتن 'to weave/compose' used metaphorically 'I wove/composed' and comes after the object and adverbial phrase in this poetic word order.
segments.s436-g11: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. مانم is 1st-person present/subjunctive 'I remain' (here 'if I remain'). اندر marks location 'in/within', and سپنجی سرای is a poetic compound 'سپنجی' modifying 'سرای' ('house/dwelling'); the whole phrase gives the condition 'if I remain in a (humble/poetic) house'.
segments.s436-g12: روان و خرد are two coordinated nouns 'soul and wisdom' (joined by و). باشدم is an optative/subjunctive form equivalent to 'may I be' or 'let it be that I am', here functioning to express a wish/result: 'may (they) be my guide'. رهنمای is the predicate noun 'guide' placed after the verb in this poetic inversion.
segments.s436-g13: سرآرم is a 1st-person verb meaning roughly 'I will set down/compose/bring to a close' (poetic/archaic); the personal pronoun من follows for emphasis. این نامه باستان is the direct object 'this ancient book/epic' (نامه = treatise/book; باستان = ancient). Note there is no object marker را here — a common poetic omission.
segments.s436-g14: به گیتی is a locative/prepositional phrase 'in the world'. بمانم is 1st-person present/subjunctive/future 'I shall remain' or 'may I remain'. یکی داستان is a predicate nominal 'as one tale' (یکی modifying داستان). The verb precedes the predicate in this inverted, poetic order.
segments.s436-g15: به نام is the formulaic opening 'in the name of'. جهاندار is an honorific/title 'world-ruler' or 'sovereign' placed before the ruler's name. محمود شاه is the king's personal name plus the title 'Shah'; word order is title → name → title as is common in classical Persian honorifics.
segments.s436-g16: ابوالقاسم is an appositive kunya (a second name) identifying the ruler. آن فر is a demonstrative + noun 'that splendor/glory' (فر = royal glory/farr). دیهیم و گاه are additional epithets; دیهیم is an archaic/adjectival epithet (uncertain sense in this context), and گاه 'time/age' is used poetically as part of the honorific phrase; و simply links the epithets.
segments.s436-g17: A nominal phrase listing the realms ruled: 'خداوند' is an honorific/sovereign noun ('the lord/sovereign') followed by three place names joined with و. Word order is head (title) + list of governed lands.
segments.s436-g18: 'ز' is the poetic preposition 'from/through'; 'فرش جهان' is a genitival/possessive phrase 'the ground/floor of the world'; 'شد' is the simple past/present-stative 'became/rose' (3rd sg.); 'چو' = 'like/as'; 'رومی پرند' is a simile 'a Roman bird/winged one.' The clause literally says 'from the world's floor he became, like a Roman bird.'
segments.s436-g19: 'به' here marks instrument/means ('by/through'); 'بخشش' = 'giving/generosity' (the means); 'همی' is the old/poetic continuous/habitual marker placed before the verb; 'گنج' is the direct object 'treasure/wealth'; 'بپراگند' is the present/habitual form of پراکندن with the prefix بـ (b-) meaning 'he scatters/was scattering.' Together: 'by giving he continually scattered treasure.'
segments.s436-g20: 'به دانایی' is an adverbial phrase 'by/with wisdom'; 'از گنج' ('from the treasure') indicates source; 'نام' ('name') stands as the theme being affected; 'آگند' is a predicative adjective/participle meaning 'filled/replete.' The clause (elliptical without an explicit verbal auxiliary) means 'by wisdom his name was filled from the treasure (i.e. he gained fame through his wealth and wisdom).'
segments.s436-g21: 'بزرگست' is a nominal/predicative clause 'it is great' (an archaic fused form = بزرگ + است). 'و' links clauses. 'چون سالیان بگذرد' is a temporal subordinate clause: 'چون' introduces 'when' (here), 'سالیان' = 'years', and 'بگذرد' is 3rd‑sg. present/subjunctive of گذشتن 'to pass' used for the temporal 'when years have passed.'
segments.s436-g22: 'ازو' = 'of it / concerning it' (prepositional phrase pointing back to the great thing); 'گوید' is the 3rd‑sg. present of گفتن 'says/speaks' (here 'will speak of'); 'آنکس که دارد خرد' is a restrictive relative clause 'the one who has wisdom' (آنکس = that person; که دارد خرد = who possesses wisdom). Together: 'the one who has wisdom will speak of it.'
segments.s436-g23: A series of ablative/instrumental phrases introduced by repeated 'ز' ('from/by'): 'ز رزم' (from battle), 'و ز بزم' (and from feasting/banquet), 'و ز بخش' (and from gifts/generosity), 'و شکار' (and the hunt). The repetition emphasizes the multiple sources/activities contributing to the state that follows.
segments.s436-g24: Another 'ز' ('through/because of') introduces the cause: 'دادش' = داد + ـش (his justice/justice of him). 'جهان شد' = 'the world became' (شد = became). 'چو' = 'as/like', and 'خرم بهار' = 'a joyful/lush spring' (خرم qualifying بهار). Altogether: 'through his justice the world became like a joyful spring.'
segments.s436-g1: This is a single noun phrase introduced by the indefinite/identifying particle یکی ('a certain'). پیر is the head noun meaning an old man; بد نامش is a genitive/attributive phrase literally 'of bad name' = 'ill‑reputed' (ش = his). آزاد سرو is a compound epithet/name applied to him (here functioning as his byname). The whole string forms one appositive description.
segments.s436-g2: که introduces a relative clause 'who'. با + name gives accompaniment 'with Ahmad Sahl'. بودی is the past copula used here in classical/poetic Persian as a third‑person past 'was'. به + place names is used here for location 'in Merv' (poetic locative).
segments.s436-g3: This is a sequence of nominal phrases using the formula Xِ/ـی + پرِ ز + Y = 'X full of Y'. دلی (heart) is followed by پر ز دانش 'full of knowledge', then سری (head) with پر سخن 'full of speech'. Word order is noun + predicate 'full of' + object. The phrases are paratactic (stacked attributes).
segments.s436-g4: زبان ('tongue' / 'speech') is the noun, followed by the same construction پر ز + گفتارهای کهن 'full of ancient utterances'. گفتارهای is plural with the plural suffix های and کهن is a postpositive adjective modifying it ('ancient'). The whole phrase parallels the previous nominal phrases.
segments.s436-g5: کجا is an adverb ('where') used here at the start of the clause (classically often rhetorical). نامه خسروان is a genitive construction 'the record/book of the kings' (خسروان = kings). داشتی is the past/preterite form 'you had/possessed' in classical Persian, so the clause says that the addressee possessed such a record.
segments.s436-g6: This parallel clause lists corporeal attributes: تن و پیکر is a doubled expression for 'body/figure' (joined by و for emphasis), پهلوان qualifies them ('of a hero/champion'), and داشتی repeats the past verb 'you had' to complete the parallel structure.
segments.s436-g7: به introduces the target of the tracing 'to Sam Nariman' (proper names). کشیدی + نژاد is a classical idiom 'کشیدن نژاد' = 'to draw/trace one's lineage' (کشیدی is past 'you drew/traced' and نژاد is 'lineage/descent'). The verb takes the goal introduced by به.
segments.s436-g8: بسی is an adverb 'much/many' modifying the object رزم 'battles/war'. داشتی again is the past 'you had'. The concluding phrase رستم به یاد is an adverbial/qualifying phrase 'in memory/of Rostam' or 'reminiscent of Rostam', so the whole means you had many battles that recalled Rostam (i.e., you resembled Rostam in warfare).
segments.s436-g25: خنک is a preposed predicate meaning 'blessed/pleasant' used here to say someone is fortunate. آنک is an archaic/poetic relative pronoun 'who/that' introducing the relative clause. بیند is 3rd-person singular present 'sees' (classical usage forming 'he who sees ...'). کلاه is the direct object 'cap/helmet/crown' and ورا is the enclitic pronoun 'to him/for him', marking possession or benefit (i.e. 'the cap that is his' or 'the cap for him').
segments.s436-g26: همان is a demonstrative 'that/the same' modifying the following noun. بارگاه is 'palace/royal court'; و is the coordinating 'and'; سپاه is 'army/host'. The final ورا is the same enclitic 'to him/for him', linking these nouns back to the person indicated in the previous clause (i.e. 'that palace and army belong to him').
segments.s436-g27: This clause places the objects before the verb in typical Persian: 'دو گوش و دو پای من' ('my two ears and my two feet') are the things affected, with من after پای marking possession ('my'). آهو is the subject 'the gazelle' and گرفت is the simple past 'seized/took'.
segments.s436-g28: تهی دستی is a nominal phrase meaning 'being empty-handed' or 'poverty' (an idiomatic compound); و links the two coordinated predications. سال نیرو گرفت is a short clause where سال ('the year') functions as the agent and نیرو گرفت 'took/took away strength' (گرفت past tense), i.e. 'the year took [my] strength'.
segments.s436-g29: ببستم here is an archaic/poetic past form meaning roughly 'I was afflicted' / 'I endured' (the exact root is old and can read as 'to strike/bind' in classical usage); برین گونه is an adverbial phrase 'in this manner/this way'. بدخواه modifies بخت and functions adjectivally as 'maleficent' or 'ill-wishing', so the whole phrase links the speaker's plight to fate ('I was afflicted in this way by an ill-wishing fate').
segments.s436-g30: بنالم is 1st-person present 'I lament' or 'I bewail' (classical present form). ز is the poetic preposition 'because of/from'. بخت بد is 'bad fortune' (بخت = fortune/luck, بد = bad), and و سال سخت adds 'and a harsh/difficult year' (سخت modifying سال). The clause states the cause of the lament.
segments.s436-g31: شب و روز ('night and day') is a temporal adverbial 'constantly'. خوانم is 1st-person present 'I recite/sing', and همی is the classical habitual adverb 'still/always' reinforcing continuity. آفرین here is a noun 'praise/acclamation' (object of خوانم, i.e. 'I recite praise').
segments.s436-g32: بران is a prepositional/adverbial form 'to/for that' linking the praise to its addressee. دادگر is a substantive/adjective 'just' used as 'just ruler', شهریار is 'king/sovereign', and زمین here means 'the land' — the whole phrase names the praised, 'that just king of the land.'
segments.s436-g33: همه شهر = 'the whole city / everyone in the city' (همه precedes the noun to indicate totality). با من = prepositional phrase 'with me' (با + pronoun من). بدین is the fusion of ب + دین (ب = به 'in/to', دین = 'this faith/this cause'); here it means 'in this (matter/faith)'. یاورند is a third-person plural verb (ـند ending) meaning 'they are supporters / help' and comes at the end as is normal in Persian word order.
segments.s436-g34: جز introduces an exception 'except'. آنکس که is a relative phrase 'that person who / those who'. بددین is an adjective meaning 'bad in religion / impious' modifying آنکس. و links the two qualities. بدگوهرند is a predicate (third-person plural) 'they are of bad character / base of nature.' The clause excludes these people from the previous statement.
segments.s436-g35: که تا introduces a temporal subordinate clause 'for / when / until'. او is the third-person pronoun 'he'. به تخت کیی = 'upon the Kayan (royal) throne' (به = 'to/on', تخت = 'throne', کیی = adjective 'Kayanian'). برنشست is a past tense verb 'sat upon / took his seat' (بر- prefix + نشست). The clause gives the time/context for what follows.
segments.s436-g36: در کین = 'in/with regard to vengeance' (در used as 'in' or 'with respect to'). و connects the two objects/actions. دست بدی را ببست = literal 'he bound/closed the hand of evil': دست here functions idiomatically as 'power/control' (i.e. the power of wrongdoing), را is the definite object marker, and ببست is past 'he bound/tied up' (here 'prevented/disabled').
segments.s436-g37: بپیچاند is a classical/poetic verb form (ب‑ prefix + پیچاند) used here as a third-person action 'he turned aside/checked/ensnared' — in context it means 'he curbed or punished'. آن را is the object 'that one' with را marking it definite. که بیشی کند = relative clause 'who does excess' (که = 'who/that', بیشی = 'excess/surplus', کند = present/habitual 'does'); together: 'he curbed that one who behaves excessively.'
segments.s436-g38: وگر چند is a concessive phrase 'and if / even if' (وگر = 'if / but if', چند here used in an archaic/concessive sense 'even if / however much'). بیشی again = 'excess'. ز پیشی = 'from/than precedence' (ز = poetic 'from', پیشی = 'precedence / higher rank'); کند is the verb 'he does'. The whole line concedes: 'even if he acts excessively because he has precedence/rank.'
segments.s436-g39: ببخشاید is an older/poetic third-person verb form (ب‑ prefix + بخشاید) meaning 'he pardoned / he forgave'. آن را is the object 'that one' with the object marker را. که دارد خرد = relative clause 'who has wisdom' (دارد present 'possesses', خرد = 'wisdom'). The clause states he grants pardon to the wise.
segments.s436-g40: ز اندازه روز = 'from/within the measure of the day' (ز = poetic 'from / within', اندازه = 'measure/extent/limit', روز = 'day' or 'time'). برنگذرد is a negative classical-form verb 'does not/will not pass or exceed' (بر- prefix + نگرزد/نگذرد negative sense) and here means 'does not exceed' or 'is not allowed to pass beyond' the stated limit. Together: 'he does not allow them to go beyond the proper limit of the day.'

sentences.s436-s21: I will set up a remembrance of him in the world, so that as long as people exist he will not be hidden.
sentences.s436-s22: By this letter, before the kings, before the great ones, and before the warlike horsemen.
sentences.s436-s23: All is battle and feast, and counsel and speech; a long ancient time has passed.
sentences.s436-s24: The same learning and faith and restraint and counsel; the same guidance to the other world.
sentences.s436-s29: that after my death a token of me will remain from the emperor's treasure for the proud-necked.
sentences.s436-s30: Now I turn back to the tale of the cypress — the radiant Sahl Mahan — at Merv.
sentences.s436-s25: If anything pleases them, that very day will bring him profit.
sentences.s436-s26: From those, the nobles were his memorial; that same was the companion of his days.
sentences.s436-s27: I always keep my eye on these times, so that I may obtain a dinar from the king.
sentences.s436-s28: Again I set my hope on another abode, that forgiveness may come to me from God.
sentences.s436-s5: Now I will tell what I learned from him; I wove the words one into another.
sentences.s436-s6: If I remain in a humble dwelling, may my soul and wisdom be my guide.
sentences.s436-s7: I will set down this ancient book; I shall remain in the world as a single tale.
sentences.s436-s8: In the name of the world-ruler Mahmud Shah Abu'l-Qasim, that splendor of the land and age.
sentences.s436-s9: The sovereign of Iran, Nīrān, and India rose up from the world's ground like a Roman bird.
sentences.s436-s10: By generosity he ever scattered treasure; by wisdom his name was filled from that treasure.
sentences.s436-s11: It is great; and when years pass, the one who has wisdom will speak of it.
sentences.s436-s12: From battle, from banquets, from gifts and the hunt — by his justice the world became like a joyful spring.
sentences.s436-s1: There was a certain elderly man of ill repute called Azad Sarv, who was in Merv with Ahmad Sahl.
sentences.s436-s2: A heart full of knowledge, a head full of speech, a tongue full of ancient utterances.
sentences.s436-s3: You possessed the records of the kings, and you had the body and figure of a champion.
sentences.s436-s4: You traced your lineage to Sam and Nariman; you had many battles, reminiscent of Rostam.
sentences.s436-s13: Blessed is the one who sees the cap for him; that very palace and army are his.
sentences.s436-s14: A gazelle seized my two ears and my two feet; I was left empty-handed and the year took my strength.
sentences.s436-s15: I was afflicted in this way by a maleficent fate; I lament because of ill fortune and a harsh year.
sentences.s436-s16: Night and day I continually sing praises to that just sovereign of the land.
sentences.s436-s17: The whole city stands with me in this cause, except those who are irreligious and of base character.
sentences.s436-s18: For when he took his seat on the Kayanian throne, he checked vengeance and bound the hand of wickedness.
sentences.s436-s19: He checked (or ensnared) anyone who acted with excess, even if he exceeded others in rank.
sentences.s436-s20: He pardoned anyone who possessed wisdom; he did not let them pass beyond the proper measure of the day.
